     The Numbers Business Report

Concussion (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $34,531,832Details
International Box Office $15,831,958
Worldwide Box Office $50,363,790
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,220,174
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,406,822
Total $7,626,996
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A dramatic thriller based on the true David vs. Goliath story of American immigrant Dr. Bennet Omalu, the forensic neuropathologist who made the first discovery of CTE, a football-related brain trauma, in a pro player and fought for the truth to be known. Omalu’s emotional quest puts him at dangerous odds with one of the most powerful institutions in the world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,513,749 (30.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.28 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:68.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,841 opening theaters/2,841 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $48,374,337

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2015 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: December 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Curacao)
December 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Aruba)
December 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Trinidad)
December 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Jamaica)
December 31st, 2015 (Wide) (South Africa)
Video Release: March 15th, 2016 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material including some disturbing images, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2385, 7/4/2015)
Running Time: 121 minutes
Plot point: Corporate Malfeasance, Football, Immigration, Medical and Hospitals
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, LStar Capital, Village Roadshow Productions, Scott Free Films, Shuman Company, Cara Films, Cantillon Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A dramatic thriller based on the true David vs. Goliath story of American immigrant Dr. Bennet Omalu, the forensic neuropathologist who made the first discovery of CTE, a football-related brain trauma, in a pro player and fought for the truth to be known. Omalu’s emotional quest puts him at dangerous odds with one of the most powerful institutions in the world.
